name: Virginia Avila
title: Secretary of State of Foreign and Business Affairs
bio: Virginia Avila is a multi-lingual Argentine Economist, with over 20-year experience in international business development for the private and public sector. Experienced in export promotion, negotiating tariff conditions for entry into foreign markets, developing strategic partnerships, managing projects, giving presentations and organising trade fair events. She was part of an Argentine technical team that negotiated Generalized Preference System (GPS) terms with the United States Government over strawberry imports. Recognized by the Organizations of American States under the Americas Competitiveness Exchange program for Innovation and Entrepreneurship. Co-founder of a Climate tech vertical Start-up called Reforest Latam in Argentina. Present triple helix activities: Public + Private + Academia. PUBLIC SECTOR Argentina: Secretary of State of foreign affairs and Business relations at the government of Tucumán, Argentina. ACE program Ambassador from 2019 extended until today. ACADEMIA: Professor of International Negotiations at the Master degree in foreign trade at UNT Universidad Nacional de Tucumán. Private CONSULTANCY: International business development consultant for private enterprises to enter new markets. She also was a consultant for the European Union Embassy in Argentina, at the Global Gateway program March-June 2023.

name: Gonzalo Valenci
title: Executive Director
bio: Gonzalo is the Executive Director of Córdoba Tech Cluster, that gathers 300 companies, startups, and organizations from the tech and knowledge sector. He served as the Executive Director of the Córdoba Innovar y Emprender Agency, from the Government Córdoba Province, where he previously served as Innovation Manager between 2018 and 2019. Also, he worked as Undersecretary of Technological Linkage at the Ministry of Science and Technology of the Province, and as Program Coordinator at the Secretariat of Industry within the Ministry of Production, Science and Technological Innovation of the Government of the Province of Córdoba. Gonzalo was CEO of Samply, a fintech startup focused on payments, and has also worked as an innovation consultant. In addition, he holds a specialization in entrepreneurship and innovation from the Start Tau division of Tel Aviv University, Israel. Gonzalo studied Communication Sciences at the National University of Córdoba.

name: Jorge Gentle
title: Manager, Small Business Development Centre Belize
organization: Belize Trade and Investment Development Service (BELTRAIDE)
bio: Jorge Gentle is the Manager of the Small Business Development Centre Belize (SBDCBelize), a unit of the Belize Trade and Investment Development Service (BELTRAIDE), a statutory body within the Office of the Prime Minister. BELTRAIDE has the national mandate for entrepreneurial development, and in his role, he leads national activities that support entrepreneurship development and the growth and competitiveness of micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs). He oversees advisory services, capacity-building programs, and strategic partnerships that improve market access and strengthen business performance for a database of 3,000+ active MSMEs. He also serves on various national and regional project steering committees, contributing to initiatives that advance innovation, entrepreneurship, and economic development. Jorge is a Certified Business Advisor with a Master’s Degree in Project Management and a Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, and brings over 16 years of professional experience in entrepreneurship development, strategic planning, project management, and financial management. He is also an entrepreneur and serves as an Adjunct Lecturer at the University of Belize, contributing to the development of the next generation of business leaders and entrepreneurs.

name: Nicolas Quintero-Fandino
title: Business and Investments Specialist
bio: Nicolás Quintero-Fandiño is a Business and Investment Specialist at ApexBrasil, where he drives strategic market development, fosters international partnerships, and supports foreign direct investment initiatives to expand Brazilian exports and attract global capital. In this role, he engages multinational companies, government stakeholders, and innovation ecosystem actors to identify opportunities and strengthen trade and investment flows across North and Central America and the Caribbean. Previously, Nicolás served as an Investment Officer at Invest in Bogotá, and later at ProColombia, facilitating market entry and expansion for foreign investors and advancing economic development objectives. He has experience in public affairs and international business promotion, bridging public and private interests to accelerate entrepreneurship and inclusive growth. Nicolás holds a MSc in International Development Policy from Georgetown University and a BA in International and Economic Relations from Universidad Jorge Tadeo Lozano. He was awarded the 2023 Colfuturo National scholarship and academic excellence recognitions during his studies at Georgetown University.

name: Dominic Blakely
title: Innovation and Entrepreneurship Strategist
organization: J Herbert Smith Centre for Technology Management and Entrepreneurship (University of New Brunswick)
bio: Dominic currently serves as the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Strategist in the J Herbert Smith Centre and as a Senior Advisor to the President of UNB. With 13 years of experience at the university, including roles as Chief of Staff and Development Officer, he now instructs in the Masters in Technology Management and Entrepreneurship (TME) program. Dominic manages student funds for prototyping and travel, oversees the TME Mentorship and Residence programs,. With a diverse background spanning politics, sales, and non-profits, he focuses on leveraging deep, long-lasting networks to drive innovation and achieve strategic goals.

name: Carolina Rosales
title: Executive Director
bio: I serve as Executive Director of Invest in Cartagena and Bolívar, the regional investment promotion agency working to mobilize national and foreign investment as a driver of sustainable economic development. I lead initiatives that align public policy, private sector engagement, and territorial development strategies to strengthen competitiveness, innovation, and entrepreneurship ecosystems. My work focuses on facilitating investment projects that contribute to productive diversification, job creation, and technology transfer, while supporting local governments in building investment-ready environments. Prior to this role, I worked at ProColombia, where I advised firms on export development and internationalization strategies across Colombia’s Caribbean region, supporting SME integration into global markets. I have also served as a university lecturer in international business and global marketing. I hold a Master’s degree in Innovative Business Creation and Management from the University of Valencia and degrees in International Business and Finance from Universidad Tecnológica de Bolívar.

name: Daniela Gutierrez
title: Business Climate Manager
bio: Daniela Gutierrez is Manager of Investment Climate and Aftercare at ProBarranquilla, where she leads efforts to enhance the business environment and support foreign direct investment through strategic aftercare, human capital development, and multi-stakeholder coordination. Her work focuses on strengthening regional competitiveness by aligning public policy, institutional frameworks, and private sector needs across key economic sectors. She has contributed to the development of integrated approaches to address productivity and skills gaps, foster linkages between global firms and local ecosystems, and improve the conditions for sustainable investment and business growth. Her role includes monitoring regulatory and market dynamics to inform evidence-based policies and initiatives that promote innovation and economic diversification. With over 15 years of experience, including 12 in the private sector, she has led business development and internationalization processes. Daniela holds a degree in Industrial Design and graduate studies in Business Management.

name: Luis Daniel Ramírez
title: Executive Director
bio: Luis Daniel Ramírez Alfaro is Executive Director of Yo Emprendedor, a social enterprise that democratizes access to entrepreneurship education across Central America. He leads strategic planning, fundraising (over $500K annually), and multi-sector partnerships, reaching more than 100,000 direct beneficiaries. In 2023, he founded Ruta E, a regional initiative certifying young people in entrepreneurial skills. Previously, he served as Partnership Manager, strengthening regional alliances and leading the transformation of a business competition into a sustainable, high-impact platform. He has also contributed to social innovation initiatives with Ashoka and served on scholarship and evaluation committees for academic and entrepreneurial programs. Luis Daniel holds a degree in International Relations from Universidad Nacional (Costa Rica) and completed the EPIC Entrepreneurship Program at Babson College. His work focuses on building entrepreneurial ecosystems and scaling education-driven economic development.

name: Carlos Fernando Villacreses
title: Director of Planning and International Cooperation
bio: Carlos Fernando Villacreses is Director of Planning and International Cooperation at the Provincial Government of Tungurahua and a strategy and innovation consultant at Kafevisa Consulting . He leads initiatives in economic development, international cooperation, and ecosystem strengthening, focusing on SME competitiveness, digital transformation, and entrepreneurship policy. He previously served as Ecuador's Undersecretary of MSMEs and Handicrafts and Zonal Director at the Ministry of Production, Foreign Trade, Investments and Fisheries, where he designed and implemented public policies to enhance productivity, exports, and entrepreneurial growth . Carlos is also a university professor and mentor in innovation and digital transformation programs. He holds a Master's degree in Artificial Intelligence (OBS Business School, Spain), an MBA from IDE Business School, and a BA in Marketing from Universidad San Francisco de Quito .

name: Erika Apunte Aguirre
title: Chief Commercial & Growth Officer
bio: Erika Apunte Aguirre is an accomplished Chief Commercial and Growth Officer, speaker, and professor with more than 16 years of experience leading high-impact initiatives across organizations in Latin America and the Caribbean. Throughout her career, she has driven numerous transformative projects with measurable results, combining strategic vision with a strong human-centered approach. She holds a Master's degree in Financial and Banking Management from the Universidad San Francisco de Quito and a Bachelor's degree in Business Administration from the same institution. She also earned a Diploma in Banking Management Skills from INTEC in the Dominican Republic. As a professor and mentor, she integrates agile methodologies, innovation, artificial intelligence, machine learning, cybersecurity, digital sales, and big data into her work, with the goal of accelerating exponential transformation within organizations. With a strong international background, Erika has consistently demonstrated her ability to adapt to diverse business and cultural environments. She currently serves as Chief of Global Sales – LATAM, USA & Spain at Kruger Corporation, where she leads the company's strategic sales transformation and global expansion, leveraging artificial intelligence. Firmly convinced that technology must serve a human purpose, Erika stands out as a leading woman in technology, committed to transforming business models and positively impacting lives through innovative and sustainable solutions.

name: Alba Sánchez
title: Deputy Director of Economic Affairs
organization: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of El Salvador
bio: Alba Sánchez is the Deputy Director of Economic Relations at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of El Salvador, where she leads strategic economic diplomacy initiatives to promote investment, trade, and innovation-driven growth. She has played a key role in advancing foreign direct investment, supporting the internationalization of enterprises, and coordinating high-impact partnerships with multilateral institutions and private sector actors. Her work includes overseeing international agreements, mobilizing development financing, and facilitating business ecosystems that foster entrepreneurship and economic development. Previously, she served as Head of Economic Diplomacy and as a Specialist in International Economic Policy, leading engagement with UN bodies and contributing to global economic policy processes. Her earlier experience includes development project management and data analysis in international cooperation contexts. She holds a Bachelor’s degree in Economics from UCA El Salvador and a Master’s degree in Business Intelligence from the University of Cádiz, Spain.

name: Hersson Vargas
title: Specialist in Multilateral Organizations and Economic Affairs
organization: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of El Salvador
bio: Hersson Vargas is an International Organizations and Economic Affairs Specialist at the Directorate of Economic Relations of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of El Salvador. In his current role, he supports the implementation and monitoring of the Economic Diplomacy Strategy in multilateral settings through economic policy analysis and coordination with international partners. His responsibilities include contributing to initiatives that promote economic development and investment. Prior to joining the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, he gained professional experience in multinational companies within the logistics and transportation sector, specializing in maritime, air, and land operations. He holds a Bachelor’s Degree in International Relations from the University of El Salvador and is a results-oriented professional with strong analytical skills and experience working in institutional and multicultural environments.

name: Juliana Ceballos
title: Software Engineer and Digital Business Dean
bio: Juliana Ceballos Garcia is a technology executive, academic leader, and digital transformation strategist. She currently serves as Dean at ESEN (Escuela Superior de Economía y Negocios) in El Salvador, where she leads innovation in business and software engineering education, integrating agile culture and artificial intelligence into academic programs. Juliana is also CEO and Co-Founder of TechyWe, a technology consulting firm specializing in custom software development, data governance, ERP solutions, and digital transformation for enterprises. She has extensive experience in data governance, enterprise architecture, and large-scale technology implementations, including prior work in the aviation industry and financial sector. Juliana is a recognized speaker and instructor in agile practices, AI-enabled teamwork, and business-technology alignment. Her work bridges academia and industry, preparing organizations and future leaders for digital competitiveness.

name: Maria Jose Nichols
title: President
bio: International Relations professional and Master's in International Business from Universidad Francisco Marroquín, with an MBA from Cedex. With over 15 years of experience in residential and commercial real estate across Guatemala and Central America, she is the Founding Partner and Director of her firm, specializing in real estate services, relocation, and project commercialization. She has represented both domestic and multinational companies in real estate transactions and asset management, leading high-performance teams and guiding international brands within the sector. Her passion for industry associations and strengthening the business ecosystem has led her to hold key leadership positions. She currently serves as President of CACIF Joven, re-elected for the 2025–2026 term, working to ensure that young entrepreneurs and business leaders are part of a robust business ecosystem that sustains the growth of their companies and of Guatemala. She also serves as Founding President of the Women Business Leaders Committee of the Chamber of Industry, with the goal of promoting the participation of women executives in decision-making spaces grounded in meritocracy. She is Vice President for Central America and the Caribbean of the Ibero-American Federation of Young Entrepreneurs (FIJE) for the 2025–2027 term. Previously, she served as President of the Real Estate Professionals Guild (GPI) and as a Board Member of CECOMS. Her leadership vision centers on building strong business networks and contributing to the country's development through the empowerment of the next generation of entrepreneurs. She firmly believes that industry collaboration and a strengthened business ecosystem are fundamental to Guatemala's sustainable growth — demonstrating that business success and social commitment can go hand in hand.

name: Yonnick David
title: Executive Director
bio: Executive Director of Fusion Hospitality Inc., where we have developed and operate a number of hospitality properties in Guyana. I lead corporate strategy, capital structuring, brand development, and multi-unit operations, overseeing executive leadership, financial performance, procurement systems, and investor relations. My work focuses on building scalable enterprises, strengthening local supply chains, generating employment, and positioning Guyana as a competitive tourism and culinary destination. Beyond hospitality operations, I conceptualize and lead national conferences and large-scale platforms focused on tourism development and the Orange Economy, bringing together creatives, policymakers, and private sector leaders to drive innovation and economic diversification. I also serve as Chief Commissioner of the Scout Association of Guyana, advancing youth development, leadership training, and civic engagement at a national level. I hold a Bachelor of Science in International Relations, which informs my strategic approach to governance, diplomacy, and cross-sector collaboration in advancing sustainable national growth.

name: Harold Davis
title: CEO
bio: Harold Davis is the Acting Chief Executive Officer of the Jamaica Business Development Corporation. JBDC is the leading business development support organization in the English-speaking Caribbean region serving 4,000+ MSMEs annually. The CEO provides strategic leadership and oversight in the execution of JBDC’s Strategic and Operational plans (MSME development programmes). Recent programme initiatives include: The successful development and management of a network of 15 SBDCs in Jamaica in partnership with all the major Universities on the island. Introduction of SBDCs in Jamaica facilitated by OAS support. 1. EU supported (1.7M euro) ‘Digital Jamaica’ project; facilitating the digital transformation of 2,000 MSMEs. To date 1,300 MSMEs participating. 2. Recent launch of OAS supported “JBDC Biz Wiz” Online learning platform innovation providing seven bespoke JBDC training courses to entrepreneurs. First such Jamaican platform of its kind. 3. JBDC developed and manages four incubators; Agro-processing, Essential Oils, Fashion and Gift and Craft where entrepreneurs are facilitated to innovate and produce new products in a world class FSSC facility (approx. $50M Ja value of production in last year). 4. Launching a Cosmeceutical incubator supported by World Bank. Harold Davis is a trained Industrial Engineer with over twenty-five (25) years’ experience in Industrial development and Leadership in the public and private sectors. With an MSc in Innovation, Leadership and Management from the University of York, UK, his areas of specialization include Entrepreneurship, Creative Industries, Innovation Management, Development Finance and Micro, Small and Medium-sized Entities (MSME) development strategies.

name: Carlos Alberto Martínez Cortes
title: CEO
bio: Carlos Alberto Martínez Cortes, CEO TECHBA LATAM. Sure, here's a professional version in English: As CEO of TechBA LATAM, I lead the development of international relations between Mexico and Latin American countries, with a strong focus on supporting small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). My role involves fostering innovation processes, facilitating international acceleration, and enabling access to new markets for these companies. I also oversee the design and implementation of innovation strategies aimed at strengthening regional development and informing public policy, with the ultimate goal of promoting sustainable economic growth and competitiveness across the region. I'm biomedical engineer with a financial background in venture capital, me and my team work in several strategic projects in Costa Rica, Perú and México focus in regional development. For this 2026 we are in charge of be the PROCOMER representative in México focus in direct hitting attraction.

name: Fabiola Murrieta Peralta
title: Strategic Partnerships & Government Affairs Coordination
bio: As Coordinator of Institutional Relations in the private sector, I have lead engagement with government authorities, industry associations, and key stakeholders to support initiatives related to economic development, growth and sustainable practices within the human rights benchmark. My experience focuses on strengthening public-private collaboration, monitoring regulatory and legislative initiatives and promoting projects for responsible business practices. I hold bachelor’s degrees in Economics and International Relations, which provide a multidisciplinary perspective on public policy, global economic trends, and institutional cooperation. I also had the opportunity to certify from the London School of Economics in Public Policy. I am currently studying my Master on Government and Public Policy at the Universidad Panamericana, reflecting my professional interest in fostering collaboration between the private sector and government to support inclusive economic growth.

name: Octavio Arzate
title: CEO
organization: Luhulaa Tradiciones de Mexico SA de CV
bio: Octavio Arzate CEO of Luhulaa Tradiciones de Mexico and Luhulaa Mezcal Entrepreneur project of Gastronomy, Agro Industry, Culture, Tourism and Mezcal from Oaxaca México with the objetive of preserve, enrich and give to know the original traditions of Mexico. Im looking and working to export or partnership around different countries our gastronomy products and mezcal winner in 2024 of Double Gold in SWSC San Francisco CA and mentioned in Forbes list how one 31 the best mezcals around the World. In 2015. Im working in collaboration with the Belize government to share our traditions, gastronomy, culture and help our indigenous communities to develop their towns I was created another entrepreneur project of Restaurant-Bar in Queretaro Mexico that today is turned 10 years. 2006-2019 I was Financial Manager for an Automotive Indsutry company one of most important in Mexico for developed of Ambulances, Patrols, Fire Cars and Rescue Cars for Mexico and South America and of which I was part of its board of directors . I Have many experience troughs of Business, Investments, Finance, Direction and Im consider me like a business man, entrepreneur and Leader.

name: Dr. Ernesto Stein
title: Distinguished University Professor in Public Policy
bio: Dr. Ernesto Stein is a Distinguished University Professor of Public Policy at Tecnológico de Monterrey in the School of Social Sciences and Government and the School of Government and Public Transformation. In February 2026, he was appointed Director of the BBVA–TEC Center for Trade Policy and Global Value Chains for North America. Previously, Dr. Stein served as the IDB Representative in Mexico, where he led all public- and private-sector activities of the IDB Group. Over a nearly 30-year career at the IDB, he held senior positions including Principal Economist in the Research Department and Regional Economic Advisor. Dr. Stein has also been a Growth Fellow at Harvard University’s Center for International Development. His research focuses on international trade, foreign direct investment, productive development policies, institutional economics, and political economy. He has authored or edited more than 12 books and published over 40 scholarly articles in leading academic journals.

name: Fausto Gurrea
title: Director of Strategy
bio: Fausto Gerardo Gurrea Martínez has a degree in Applied Mathematics from the Autonomous Technological Institute of Mexico. His professional experience began as a business analyst at the consulting firm McKinsey & Company, in which he collaborated in the financial sector practice, in projects related to risk analysis and management, mainly in the mortgage and pension loan market. He ventured into the public policy area by participating as a researcher and analyst at the Mexican Institute for Competitiveness (IMCO). During this time, he collaborated in the design and construction of the first international competitiveness report and the first state competitiveness study prepared by that institution and in various economic studies carried out in the area of telecommunications, rule of law and security. Subsequently, he completed a Master of Science in Operations Research from the London School of Economics and Political Science, and a Master of Public Administration and International Development from the John F. Kennedy School of Government at Harvard University. During his studies, he carried out work stays in various public, private and social sector organizations, highlighting the Water Research Council, a consulting company in the drinking water and sewerage services sector in the United Kingdom; at Root Capital, a lending organization for rural cooperative enterprises in emerging economies based in Cambridge, Massachusetts; and at International Development Enterprises (currently Proximity Designs), a company that offers solutions with private sector models to provide development opportunities for peasants in highly marginalized and poor areas with operations in Yangon, Myanmar. In the summer of 2008, he participated in the restructuring of the financial risk supervision area of the National Banking and Securities Commission. Between November 2008 and February 2013, he was part of the Undersecretary of Competitiveness and Regulations of the Ministry of Economy of the Government of the Republic, participating mainly in institutional improvement efforts to increase productivity, build quality regulation and promote economic competition, mainly in network or transversal markets such as telecommunications, transport, financial and energy. Between March 2013 and March 2014, Fausto, accompanied by his wife Ana, made a 365-day trip to more than 20 countries in the Americas, Europe, Asia and Oceania. Between 2014 and 2018, he participated as Senior Manager of Strategy Planning and Management at the Institute of the National Housing Fund for Workers (Infonavit). During this period, he was able to restructure the credit conditions to exceed the credit goals and give the highest yields to the Housing Subaccount in history.

name: Mary Angelica Perez Lopez
title: General Director
bio: Mary Angélica Pérez López is Head of the Yucatán State Development Agency, where she leads strategic planning, public policy design, and implementation to enhance regional competitiveness and economic development. She oversees the articulation of cross-sector strategies that integrate investment attraction, innovation, and productive development, aligning state-level priorities with global economic trends. She has extensive experience in structuring and managing public-private partnerships, strengthening institutional coordination, and fostering collaboration among government, private sector, academia, and international organizations. She has led international cooperation initiatives, particularly with the European Union, in areas such as circular economy, digital transformation, and innovation-driven development. She holds a Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration and a Master’s degree in Finance, and is certified in cluster management by The Cluster Competitiveness Group. Her work focuses on enabling sustainable regional growth through strategic governance, policy execution, and international partnerships.

name: Ramon Colina
title: President and CEO
bio: Ramon Colina is the General Manager of RC Contractors, Inc. and Enercom, S.A., where he leads the execution of infrastructure, water, and energy-related projects across Panama. His role focuses on strategic growth, project delivery, and building high-performance teams, with an emphasis on innovation in construction, water management, and industrial instrumentation. He has led projects for key institutions such as the Panama Canal, government entities, and private sector clients. He holds a degree in Business Administration and has over 15 years of experience in construction, procurement, and project management. He is actively developing new ventures in water technologies, healthcare distribution, and regional construction expansion, with a strong interest in entrepreneurship, operational excellence, and scalable business models.

name: Yill Otero
title: General Secretary
bio: Yill Otero Guevara has more than twenty-five years of experience in international cooperation, multilateral diplomacy, and international trade. Throughout her career, she has promoted strategic partnerships between the public sector, private sector, and international organizations, contributing to Panama’s positioning in regional and global arenas. She currently works with the Investment Attraction and Export Promotion Agency of Panama (PROPANAMA) at the Ministry of Commerce and Industries, where she supports the design and implementation of strategies to strengthen national competitiveness, expand Panama’s export offer, and attract high-impact investments that generate employment, knowledge transfer, and sustainable development. For nearly two decades, she served as Inter-Agency Coordination Officer for the United Nations System in Panama, facilitating collaboration among UN agencies, government institutions, and strategic partners to implement programs aligned with national priorities. She also served as Vice Minister for Multilateral Affairs and Cooperation at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and has held several senior leadership roles in government. In 2024, she was decorated by the ACE Program as Ambassador Ad Honorem, recognizing the success achieved through the program’s edition held in Panama that year. She holds an MBA with a specialization in Marketing and postgraduate studies in Project Management, Leadership, and Coaching, among others.

name: Ignacio Cazana
title: Alternate Representative
organization: Permanent Mission of Paraguay to the OAS
bio: Ignacio Cazana currently serves as Alternate Representative of Paraguay to the Organization of American States (OAS). In this role, he leads on development-related issues and manages the Mission’s concurrent accreditation with CARICOM Member States, contributing to policy dialogue, regional cooperation, and initiatives that promote economic development, innovation, and entrepreneurship. His work involves coordination across multilateral and bilateral agendas, as well as engagement in negotiations and programs that strengthen trade and institutional capacity. Previously, he served as a Foreign Service officer at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, with experience in the Legal Advisory Office on Public International Law and in the Directorate of Economic Integration, where he participated in negotiations on economic rules within MERCOSUR. He holds an LL.M. in International Law, Trade, and Investment.

name: Linda Taiyen
title: Director
organization: Camara de Comercio y Servicios de Ciudad del Este
bio: Linda Taiyen is a legal and institutional professional in Paraguay with a solid track record in strategic leadership and regional development. She serves as Legal Director of the Chamber of Commerce and Services of Ciudad del Este, and Vice President of the Council for the Development of Ciudad del Este (CODELESTE). In these roles, she promotes public-private coordination, strengthens the business climate, and enhances the legal framework for trade, investment, and regional competitiveness. She has advanced strategic initiatives such as an investment hub, a Regional Logistics Hub, and interinstitutional dialogue platforms integrating the public, private, and academic sectors. She leads strategic planning processes, provides regulatory advisory services, and designs proposals aimed at institutional modernization and capital attraction. She also coordinates interinstitutional cooperation efforts, participates in public policy advocacy spaces, and develops technical initiatives that contribute to sustainable development, territorial competitiveness, and the strengthening of the business environment in Alto Paraná.

name: Daniel Ybarra
title: Secretary General
bio: Daniel Ybarra is a Mechanical and Industrial Engineer with a postgraduate diploma in Foreign Trade. He possesses a multidisciplinary background focused on value creation and business strengthening. He currently serves as General Secretary of the Association of Young Entrepreneurs (AJE Peru), an affiliate of FIJE. In this leadership role, he spearheads initiatives to bolster the entrepreneurial ecosystem and enhance employability across the Ibero-American region. In addition to founding the construction firm FIXCORE, he holds executive roles at RIDAX CORP, managing brands such as Whootrips, Itertrips, and Yashtay. His primary social impact project focuses on the tourism sector, where he develops alternative economic models alongside rural Peruvian communities. This work transforms natural resources and landscapes into sustainable products that stimulate local economies. Furthermore, he contributes to the communications sector through strategic audiovisual production, integrating innovation and commercial vision to foster regional economic growth.

name: Roberto Chevarria
title: Vice Presidente Andino
organization: FIJE - Federación Iberoamericana de Jóvenes Empresarios
bio: Roberto Chevarria is the General Manager of Whootrips and RIDAX CORP SAC, where he oversees strategic operations and international expansion for high-level tourism and destination management. With over 23 years of experience in the travel industry, he specializes in optimizing tour operator logistics and fostering economic growth through sustainable tourism models. Currently, Roberto serves as the President of AJE Peru and Andean Vice President of FIJE, leading initiatives that empower young entrepreneurs and promote cross-border business innovation. A key highlight of his recent work includes organizing the ‘Growth or Crisis’ forum, which brought together 100 business leaders to navigate emerging economic challenges. He holds a deep expertise in managing complex professional delegations and industrial internships across Europe and the Americas. Roberto is committed to driving economic development by bridging international markets and mentoring the next generation of Latin American business leaders.

name: Mikhail Bullard
title: Minister Counsellor / Alternate Representative
organization: Permanent Mission of The Bahamas to the OAS
bio: Mikhail Bullard was born in Nassau, The Bahamas and has received a Bachelor with Dbl Honors in Criminology and Economics from St. Thomas University in Canada, a Master of Public Administration from Queens University in Canada and a Master of Science in Finance from Georgetown University in Washington DC. He has 12 years of experience in the Bahamian public and diplomatic service with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, including postings at the Embassy of the Bahamas in Washington DC and Permanent Mission of The Bahamas to the OAS, respectively. He is currently Minister Counsellor and Deputy in the Permanent Mission of The Bahamas to the OAS. During his postings, his portfolio has included coverage of, and the development and coordination of initiatives related to, the areas of human, economic and sustainable development. He also has 3 years of experience working as an international public servant at the Organization of American States in Washington DC in the Executive Secretariat for Integral Development (SEDI) supporting the creation and execution of partnerships for development in Latin America and the Caribbean.

name: Morgan Gilbert
title: Director of Strategic Planning & Innovation
organization: Ministry of Economic Affairs, Government of The Bahamas
bio: Morgan J. Gilbert is Director of Strategic Planning & Innovation at the Ministry of Economic Affairs, Government of The Bahamas, the main body for national policy and economic growth. She provides strategic oversight and collaborates across sectors to identify opportunities, lead initiatives, and develop solutions that inform Executive Leadership on immediate and long-term goals. Ms Gilbert distils qualitative insights into data-driven policy. She constructs initiatives into business cases that advance national competitiveness, entrepreneurship, and efficiency. Collaborating with public and private stakeholders, she ensures executive actions align with national priorities, sustainable growth, digital transformation, business ease, and domestic investment. She is currently implementing frameworks and programs for emerging sectors related to the Blue and Green Economies, and elevates public-sector standards. By emphasising evidence-based decisions, resilience, and sustainability, she strengthens The Bahamas' capacity and reputation for innovation and economic development. Ms Gilbert earned an M.S. in Chemistry, a B.S. in Biochemistry, and certifications in project management and process optimisation. She advances institutional strategic planning, project management, risk management, performance evaluation, data analysis, and government relations.

name: Ido Sarig
title: Director, Technology Commercialization
bio: Ido Sarig is Director of Technology Commercialization at the University of Memphis, where he leads efforts to translate research into market-driven ventures. He founded the university’s Venture Studio, secured state innovation funding, and has helped launch multiple startups based on university IP while negotiating licensing agreements that connect academia with industry. Previously, Ido served as Managing Director of Alchemist Accelerator’s Memphis hub and as a Venture Partner, where he invested in and mentored early-stage companies, guiding founders on product-market fit, go-to-market strategy, and scaling. With over 20 years of experience spanning venture capital, corporate development, and startup operations, he has built and exited companies, led innovation-driven business units, and supported dozens of entrepreneurs. His work sits at the intersection of economic development and innovation, fostering high-growth startups, commercializing technology, and strengthening regional entrepreneurial ecosystems.

name: Adriana Bonilla
title: Assistant Director of Business Development
bio: Adriana serves as the Assistant Director for Business Development at Arlington Economic Development (AED), situated in one of the most vibrant hubs in the United States, just minutes away from Washington, D.C. In her role, she fosters partnerships between Arlington and international entities, connecting businesses with key players in sectors such as AI, aerospace, cybersecurity, and fintech. Prior to this, she worked at the Organization of American States (OAS) for over 15 years, where she spearheaded strategic programs and built cooperation networks. Adriana is passionate about women’s leadership and promoting Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI).

name: Alma Vanessa Peralta
title: International Trade & Investment Specialist
organization: City of Tucson / Arizona District Export Council
bio: Alma Peralta is an International Trade and Investment Specialist in the City Manager's Office with the City of Tucson', where she works to position the region as a competitive destination for foreign direct investment and supports local companies in accessing global markets. In her role, she leads international delegations, trade missions, and binational forums that strengthen cross-border commerce and economic collaboration. Alma serves as a member of the Arizona District Export Council and the Arizona Advisory Committee for the U.S. Global Leadership Coalition. She has been a featured speaker at the SelectUSA Mexico Conference and was recognized as a Tucson 40 Under 40 honoree. Her work has received national recognition, including the IEDC Gold Award and the AAED Golden Prospector Award for the Academy for International Trade & Investment. She holds an MBA from the University of Phoenix and a BA in Economics from the University of Arizona, and is a certified Arizona Economic Development Professional.

name: Erika Dillard
title: CEO
bio: Erika Dillard is a physician-entrepreneur and CEO of PopCheck Technologies, Inc., an early-stage medical device startup developing AI-enabled remote monitoring solutions for surgical complications like venous clots. At PopCheck, she leads the strategic vision, product development, and commercialization efforts to enhance patient outcomes and reduce hospital readmissions through innovative, non-invasive wearable technology. Dr. Dillard earned her MD/PhD from the University of Tennessee Health Science Center, where she developed oncolytic viral therapies for brain tumors. She trained in neurological surgery and neuro-critical care, gaining expertise in patient safety related to post-surgical complications. She further developed innovation and commercialization skills through the Patents2Products postdoctoral fellowship at the University of Memphis. As an advocate for workforce development in healthcare and biotechnology, she served as Past President of BioTN MidSouth Academic Alliance, supporting Tennessee’s life science workforce, and as an Appointee for the Council on Concerns of Women Physicians within the National Medical Association.

name: Jillian Friot
title: General Partner
bio: Jillian Friot is a venture ecosystem builder focused on connecting founders, investors, and innovation leaders across regions to accelerate economic growth. She is the co-founder of Venture Builders Fund, an initiative that invests in early-stage startups emerging from accelerators, venture studios, and university entrepreneurship programs while strengthening the ecosystems that support them. Previously, Jillian served as CEO of the Global Venture Network (GVN), a community of more than 300 venture organizations across 50+ countries supporting over 26,000 startups. Earlier in her career at Dassault Systemes, she created and scaled the global SOLIDWORKS for Startups program from launch to more than 20,000 startups worldwide, working closely with accelerators, universities, and economic development organizations. Jillian has mentored founders across multiple accelerators globally and serves on the board of Fierce Foundry Venture Studio. She is based in Memphis, Tennessee, where she is actively engaged in strengthening innovation and entrepreneurship locally and across the Americas.

name: Mark Wells
title: Director of Global Trade and Investment
bio: Mark Wells is Director of Global Trade and Investment at the Oklahoma Department of Commerce, where he leads the state’s Global Engagement Team. In this role, he promotes Oklahoma as a destination for foreign direct investment, supports export growth for Oklahoma companies, and works with international partners to generate high-quality jobs and strengthen the state’s innovation and industrial ecosystem. Prior to joining Oklahoma Commerce, Wells served more than 30 years as a U.S. diplomat with the U.S. Department of State. His senior assignments included Principal Deputy Assistant Secretary in the Bureau of Western Hemisphere Affairs and Deputy Assistant Secretary for South America and regional economic policy. Overseas, he served as Deputy Chief of Mission in Bogotá, Colombia, and Lima, Peru, with earlier postings in Colombia, Venezuela, Iraq, Bolivia, and Mexico. Wells holds master’s degrees from the National War College and George Washington University and a bachelor’s degree in government from Oral Roberts University. He speaks fluent Spanish.

name: Patrick Van Pelt
title: Venture Studio Founder & Fiduciary Steward
bio: Patrick Van Pelt is a venture studio founder and fiduciary leader focused on building integrated systems that advance economic resilience, health, and long-term value creation. As Managing Member of Altravia Venture Studio and Manager of Longevity Fiduciary, he oversees governance, investment advisory, and the development of innovation platforms such as Rebalansa, a compliance and health-plan optimization system. His work centers on aligning finance, care, and enterprise strategy—designing structures that improve performance while strengthening outcomes for organizations and their stakeholders. Patrick brings over 35 years of experience advising closely held businesses, institutions, and multi-generational enterprises across the United States. He holds an MBA from Rice University and a BS in Finance from Marquette University. His leadership integrates entrepreneurship, fiduciary governance, and system design to support sustainable growth and innovation across sectors.

name: Radin Rahimzadeh
title: Founder, CEO
bio: Radin Rahimzadeh is an entrepreneur, investor, podcast host, and advocate whose work sits at the convergence of sustainable technology, equitable innovation, and global policy. Rooted in California and engaged across the world, she is deeply committed to ensuring that the benefits of innovation reach those who have historically been left at the margins. Radin is the founder and CEO of City-Scan AI, a platform that empowers cities to aggregate and interpret their data through visual reports, forecasts, and securitized collaborative workspaces, giving municipalities the tools to make faster, more informed, and more equitable decisions. Her entrepreneurial path spans over a decade of building at the frontier of transportation and Smart City technologies. As the founder and CEO of Fore Transit, a Forbes 30 Under 30-recognized company, she and her team demonstrated that sustainability and scalability are not competing priorities with their patented devices for heavy-duty vehicles that measurably reduce energy consumption and emissions. Prior to Fore Transit, Radin was a global consultant at AMG, deploying Autonomous Vehicle and Smart City technologies with communities to meet United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. A sought-after voice on the future of sustainable innovation, she has been featured on MSNBC and has presented at major technology conferences and at COP29, bringing her perspective on equitable climate solutions to international audiences at the highest levels of global policy discourse. Her work as an investor reflects the same values that animate her entrepreneurship. She seeks out founders driven by intentionality and conviction, particularly those working to expand access, reduce friction, and advance equity in entrenched industries. As an Iranian-American woman who has built at the frontier of technology and sustainability, Radin understands firsthand that progress requires not only bold ideas, but the full participation of voices that have too often been excluded from the table. Radin co-hosts Sprezzatura, a podcast dedicated to thoughtful, unhurried dialogue with the thinkers, founders, and creators shaping the cities and industries we inhabit. Each season of Sprezzatura is set in a different global city, following the life journeys of guests from diverse fields and backgrounds through conversations that illuminate the values, decisions, and defining moments behind their work.

name: Steve Galbraith
title: CEO
bio: Steve Galbraith is Founder and CEO of Tappy, an AI infrastructure and workforce training company that builds locally deployed AI systems to drive economic development in underserved communities. Tappy specializes in making advanced AI accessible and affordable — deploying local compute infrastructure, fine-tuning models at 90% lower cost than enterprise solutions, and designing voice-enabled AI interfaces that allow non-technical workers in manufacturing, healthcare, and trades to use AI hands-free. Steve brings a background in economics and capital markets with 15+ years building AI and education technology systems. Tappy has reached 100,000+ students through AI-enhanced learning platforms and maintains an AWS cloud infrastructure partnership. The company has been developing production AI systems since 2015. Steve is currently partnering with CoCreate LLC and Tech Ranch on an AI economic development initiative in Colorado targeting workforce training, AI infrastructure deployment, and company attraction for the region.

name: Suzanne Drumwright
title: COO / Co-founder
bio: Suzanne is passionate about making automation a reality in industries that rely on manual labor to perform grueling, repetitive tasks. A successful entrepreneur for over a decade, she first served clients across a variety of industries with marketing and brand strategy before discovering her passion for using robotics to empower people and improve the future of work. Over the past several years, Suzanne has immersed herself in the logistics industry to deeply understand warehouse workflows, labor challenges, and how automation can be seamlessly integrated into real-world operations. Her work focuses on developing robotic solutions that integrate into existing environments without requiring infrastructure changes, with safety and peak performance as top priorities. She is partnered with a technical co-founder who brings more than 25 years of experience advancing the state of the art in robotics alongside some of the industry’s leading organizations, combining deep technical expertise with a strong focus on real-world adoption.

name: Venki Mandapati
title: Co-Founder
bio: Venki Mandapati is the co-founder of UpSquad, an innovative technology platform designed to connect individuals with opportunities that foster personal and professional growth. In this role, he leads product innovation, data-driven decision-making, and strategic partnerships to enhance educational and workforce development solutions. His work focuses on leveraging technology to empower communities, drive economic development, and support entrepreneurship. Previous Work Experience & Academic Achievements: With over 12 years of experience as a Data Scientist, Venki has worked with major corporations like FedEx and AutoZone in the US, where he specialized in data analytics, predictive modeling, and building AI Applications to drive operational efficiency. Academically, Venki holds: Master’s degree in Entrepreneurship from the University of Pennsylvania M.S. in Information Systems from the University of Memphis MBA from the University of Memphis His contributions to innovation and leadership have been recognized widely, including being named University of Memphis’s Top 20 Under 30 in 2020, Growth Driver Award recipient from the Nashville Entrepreneurship center in 2022.

name: Bryan T. Daniels
title: President & CEO
bio: Bryan Daniels leads the Blount Partnership, a multidisciplined economic development agency located in the Smoky Mountains of Tennessee. The Blount Partnership operates in the fields of workforce development, business retention and expansion, research and development, business recruitment, entrepreneurship, and tourism development. He currently serves as the past chairman of the International Economic Development Council (IEDC).

name: Dave Eyerly
title: CEO / Founder
bio: Dave Eyerly is the CEO and Founder of AcceleratePV, a business accelerator in Florida. He works with investors, entrepreneurs, and service providers to help startups succeed through classes, pitch events, networking opportunities, and investor introductions. AcceleratePV is also developing a soft landing program for companies looking to expand to the U.S. Dave previously founded Surf Air, a membership-based airline in California. He holds a degree in Aeronautical Science from Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University in Daytona Beach.

name: Senofer E. Mendoza
title: Founder, General Partner
bio: Senofer Mendoza is not just a name in the venture capital (VC) world; she’s a trailblazer actively reshaping the landscape with her commitment to innovation, fostering the next generation of entrepreneurs, and investing in talent across the whole of the United States. As the Founder and General Partner of Mendoza Ventures, established in 2016, Senofer focuses on investing in dual-use companies in AI and Cybersecurity, but her impact extends far beyond the realm of technology. Since its founding seven years ago, Mendoza Ventures has raised two funds and had four successful exits. In 2022, she was appointed to the National Advisory Council on Innovation and Entrepreneurship (NACIE), where her expertise and insights contribute to shaping national policy and fostering an innovation ecosystem designed to benefit the entire nation. By advising the United States Department of Commerce and Economic Development Administration on best practices, this committee wrote the country’s National Entrepreneurship Strategy, and recommended the creation of E.D.A.’s TechHUBS program. This committee drove the idea that entrepreneurship is a hallmark of American competitiveness, and that it should be everywhere, for all Americans.

name: Ignacio Munyo
title: CEO
bio: Ignacio Munyo is Executive Director of CERES, Uruguay's leading think tank and one of the top-ranked institutions in Latin America for interdisciplinary research. He leads fundraising efforts, manages a multidisciplinary research team, and develops partnerships with leading institutions worldwide. He oversees the production of research publications and policy reports, provides regular analysis on economic and political developments that reach strong media impact. In this role, he maintains permanent and direct engagement with the sitting government as well as with political leaders, labor unions, and the business community across the country. He has over two decades of experience in international consulting. Munyo is Professor at the University of Montevideo, where he teaches MBA courses in Economics and Non-Market Strategies. His academic work has been published in leading international journals. He holds a PhD in Economics from Universidad de San Andrés and an MA in Economics from the University of Chicago.

name: Stephen Hayton
title: Founder
bio: Steve is one of the most respected operators in the accelerator and venture-building world. As former Chief Experience Officer at the Global Venture Network (GVN), he architected the organization's founder development frameworks, accelerator methodologies, and global programming — widely adopted playbooks used by venture organizations across North America, Europe, Asia, and Latin America. He has coached hundreds of founders and venture leaders, designed programs that have supported companies through millions in follow-on capital, and shaped how innovation organizations measure success. His work bridges behavioral science, venture modeling, and operational rigor — making him exceptionally skilled at evaluating founder readiness, startup resilience, and early inflection points. Steve's gift is spotting teams with coachability, grit, and execution capacity long before traditional investors can. His pattern recognition, diligence capability, and people-first investment lens provide the fund with a sharp, evidence-backed advantage in sourcing and underwriting early-stage opportunities from structured programs.

name: Samuel Moreno Gonzalez
title: Senior Specialist
bio: Samuel is an international development economist with 13+ years of experience in international trade, investment, regional integration, and private sector engagement. He currently holds the position of Senior Specialist at the Inter-American Development Bank’s Productivity, Trade and Innovation Sector. His current role involves being the project leader for the BID for the Americas Program, an IDB program designed to strengthen economic ties and expand business opportunities between Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC) and companies across IDB non-borrowing member countries, like the U.S.A. Samuel holds a Master of Science in Applied Economics from The Johns Hopkins University and a Bachelor of Arts in Economics and International Affairs from The George Washington University.

name: Santiago Pastrana
title: Consultant
bio: Santiago is a lawyer with a Bachelor of Laws from the Universidad Complutense of Madrid and a Master of Science in Foreign Service (MSFS), with honors, in International Relations and Security from Georgetown University. He has extensive experience working in multilateral institutions, including the World Bank Group and the Inter-American Development Bank (IDB). During his time at the IDB, Santiago worked closely with the Bank’s former President, Luis Alberto Moreno, and the Vice Presidency for the Private Sector. He also contributed to the Inter-American Investment Corporation (IIC), now BID Invest, where he supported the equity investment team in the development of initiatives focused on small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). In addition, Santiago served as Alternate Executive Director at the World Bank Group, representing a diverse constituency—including Colombia, Brazil, and other countries—on the Boards of the IBRD, IFC, IDA, and MIGA. Since 2023, he has worked as a consultant in the Productivity, Trade, and Innovation (PTI) Sector of the IDB, which focuses on strengthening competitiveness and productivity across Latin America and the Caribbean. In this role, Santiago has been actively involved in promoting the BID for the Americas program, an initiative aimed at fostering economic opportunities and deepening economic ties among 26 countries in Latin America and the Caribbean and key partners in Europe, North America, and Asia.
